name: Western Union
description_short: Western Union at 555 E Grant Rd, Tucson, AZ 85705 in the Keeling neighborhood is open 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM daily. Call +1 520-622-1976.
description_long:
Western Union is an established international money transfer company operating primarily through a network of retail agent locations rather than as a standalone financial institution. The company has pivoted from its historical telegraph origins to become a leading provider of cross-border and domestic money movement services.

Western Union's core offerings include: domestic and international money transfers with online initiation and in-store pickup, bill payment services, money order issuance, mobile top-up services for prepaid phones, prepaid card products, and inmate payment services. Customers can start transfers online 24/7 and complete payments at physical agent locations during business hours. The company also offers Western Union Rewards loyalty program, a tracking system for active transfers, and multi-language support (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese).

The company's primary distinction is its extensive retail agent network rather than direct banking infrastructure. Western Union operates through thousands of retail partners including Walmart, Safeway, City Market, Circle K, and Kroger locations, providing widespread physical access. This agent-based model enables convenience for unbanked and underbanked populations while maintaining operational efficiency. The website supports multiple languages and offers both digital initiation and in-person completion options.

The primary limitation is lack of transparency on the website regarding fees, exchange rates, and specific pricing—critical factors for money transfer consumers. The website does not prominently display competitive rate information, APRs, or detailed service terms, requiring customers to contact locations or use the price estimator tool. Additionally, service availability varies by location (as evidenced by the "Money Order Services only" limitation at some branches), and the website's navigation structure creates barriers to finding comprehensive service information.
pros: 24/7 online money transfer initiation through WU.com website
Extensive agent network with 150+ U.S. locations including major retailers (Walmart, Safeway, City Market)
Multi-language support in 6 languages including Spanish, Haitian Creole, Vietnamese, and French
Multiple payment methods and delivery options including card-based receiving and mobile top-up
Western Union Rewards loyalty program for frequent users
Specialized services including inmate payments and prepaid card options
Real-time transfer tracking system available online
cons: No transparent fee or exchange rate information displayed on website—customers must contact locations or use price estimator
Service limitations vary by location (e.g., some branches offer money orders only, not full transfer services)
No online account dashboard visible; requires login but minimal transparency on account features or historical data retention
Website contains 404 errors and navigation issues that impede finding service details and pricing
Limited information about sending limits, delivery timeframes, or eligibility requirements on the public website
best_for: Unbanked and underbanked consumers needing domestic money movement without a bank account
Immigrants sending remittances to family members in other countries
Consumers preferring in-person transactions and physical retail locations over digital-only providers
diagnosis: Western Union is best for unbanked consumers and international remittance senders who value physical retail access over digital convenience, and for those in underserved communities lacking traditional banking infrastructure. The main caveat is that pricing and fee structures are intentionally opaque on their website—customers must contact specific agent locations or use their estimator tool to understand actual costs, making comparison shopping difficult.
services: Domestic money transfers with online initiation
Mobile top-up for prepaid cell phones
Prepaid card products and account receiving
